zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret Fisher於1562年6月27日出生在英國英格蘭坎布利其爾格亞特 格藍斯登時，她的父親Thomas Guy Fisher20歲，母親Jone Sutton23歲。她於1581年與Robert Treadway在英國亨廷格多史爾巴登結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。她去世於1637年在英國越可爾莫爾頓享年75歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret Fisher于1562年6月27日出生在英国英格兰坎布利其爾格亞特 格藍斯登时，她的父亲Thomas Guy Fisher20岁，母亲Jone Sutton23岁。她于1581年与Robert Treadway在英国亨廷格多史爾巴登结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。她去世于1637年在英国越可爾莫爾頓享年75岁。

nameMeanings.name: Fisher
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name for a fisherman, from Middle English fis(sc)her(e) ‘fisherman’ (Old English fiscere). In North America, this surname has absorbed cognates from many other languages, including German Fischer and its Slavic(ized) variant Fišer (see Fiser ), Dutch Visser , Hungarian Halász (see Halasz ), Italian Pescatore , Slovenian Ribič (see Ribic ), and Croatian Ribić or Ribar .

English: in a few cases, possibly a topographic name for someone who lived near a fish weir on a river, from Middle English fis(sc)hwere, fisshyar ‘fish weir’ (Old English fiscwer, fiscgear), or a habitational name from a place so named, such as Fisher in North Mundham, Sussex.
Irish: translation into English of Gaelic Ó Bradáin ‘descendant of Bradán’, a personal name meaning ‘salmon’. See Braden .

variants.name: Fischer
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Hans, Otto, Erwin, Gerhard, Helmut, Fritz, Klaus, Horst, Dieter, Franz, Ernst.

German, Danish, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): occupational name for a fisherman, from Middle High German visch, German Fisch ‘fish’ + the agent suffix -er. This form of the surname is also established in some other parts of Europe, notably in France (Alsace and Lorraine), Hungary, and the Netherlands. In Poland, Czechia, Croatia, and Slovenia it is (also) established in the Slavicized forms Fiszer (Polish) and Fišer (see Fiser ). Compare Fisher .

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Fisher was born on 27 June 1562, in Great Gransden, Cambridgeshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Thomas Guy Fisher, was 20 and her mother, Jone Sutton, was 23. She married Robert Treadway in 1581, in Buckden, Huntingdonshire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died in 1637, in Moulton, Yorkshire, England, at the age of 75.

Elle a épousé Robert Treadway en 1581, à Buckden, Huntingdonshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Elle est décédée en 1637, à Moulton, Everwyck, Angleterre, à l’âge de 75 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Fisher(이)가 1562년 6월 27일 영국 잉글랜드 캄브리지실르 그리트 그랜즈든에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Thomas Guy Fisher(은)는 20세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Jone Sutton(은)는 23세였다. 그녀는 1581년 영국 헌틴그도실르 벅든에서 Robert Treadway(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1637년 영국 요르크실르 모우턴에서 75세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret Fisher родилась 27 июня 1562 г. в местности грит грансден, камбриджешир, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Thomas Guy Fisher было 20 лет, а матери, которую звали Jone Sutton, 23. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Robert Treadway в 1581 г. в местности буккден, хантингдоншир, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Она умерла в 1637 г. в местности моултон, еркшир, Англия в возрасте 75 лет.
